% auto-generated: throughput vs application-side CPU, deep/nested fetch on PostgreSQL
\begin{figure}[htbp]
  \centering
  \begin{tikzpicture}
    \begin{axis}[
      width=\linewidth, height=6.2cm,
      xlabel={Application-side CPU utilization (\%)}, ylabel={Throughput (req/s)},
      xmin=0, xmax=560, ymin=0, ymax=4200, xtick={0,100,200,300,400,500},
      grid=major, grid style={gray!20},
      nodes near coords, point meta=explicit symbolic,
      every node near coord/.append style={anchor=west, xshift=2pt, font=\footnotesize},
    ]
      \addplot[scatter, only marks, mark=*, scatter src=explicit symbolic] coordinates {(100,3529) [pg] (100,2441) [knex] (100,2060) [drizzle] (465,3506) [prisma] (105,1082) [sequelize] (105,1246) [typeorm] (105,977) [objection] (105,559) [mikroorm]};
    \end{axis}
  \end{tikzpicture}
  \caption{Throughput versus application-side CPU on the deep/nested fetch (PostgreSQL, ten-connection pool). Prisma reaches native-tied throughput at roughly five times the CPU of every other layer; the data-mapper ORMs are low on both axes. CPU is the server process only; database-side CPU is excluded for all layers alike.}
  \label{fig:cpu_tradeoff}
\end{figure}
